metadata.agency: Ministry of the Interior of the Czech Republic
metadata.program: Programu bezpecnostniho vyzkumu CR 2021-2026: vyvoj, testovani a evaluace novych bezpecnostnich technologii (SECTECH) - II. verejna soutez

abstract.label: Abstract
abstract.text: Deepfakes are a new application of artificial intelligence with a significant impact on computer security. They are becoming easier than ever to create, and these tools can be used to deceive individuals and organizations, especially when combined with social engineering tactics such as phishing. This project therefore focuses on understanding the possible uses and associated threats in the malicious use of voice deepfakes, with the intention of finding reliable strategies to mitigate these threats. By doing so, we can ensure that these innovations are used ethically and responsibly, creating a more sustainable and fairer future for all. Its activities are focused on three main areas: enhancing the robustness of voice biometric systems against voice deepfake spoofing attacks, creating tools to counter the malicious use of voice deepfakes, and evaluating people's ability to recognize voice deepfakes, while also finding ways to increase users' security awareness.
